mnemonics.title: Types of Obsolescence
mnemonics.content: TEEP - Technical, Economic, Environmental (impacts), Planned - helps remember major categories

mnemonics.title: Sustainable IT 3Rs
mnemonics.content: Reduce, Reuse, Recycle - core principle for managing obsolescence

mnemonics.title: Green Procurement Criteria
mnemonics.content: EERC - Energy efficiency, Environmental impact, Recyclability, Compliance

acronyms.usage: Policy holding manufacturers accountable for end-of-life product management

acronyms.expansion: Electronic Waste Assessment and Sustainable Treatment

acronyms.usage: Includes acquisition, maintenance, and disposal costs helping assess obsolescence economics

visualTips.visualization: Draw a circular diagram: New Product → Use → Obsolescence → Disposal → New Product. Show where backward compatibility breaks the cycle.
Create a flowchart: Manufacturing → Consumer Use → Disposal → (Landfill OR Recycling). Highlight the recycling path for sustainability.
Compare two block diagrams: Modular shows independent upgradeable blocks; Monolithic shows single unit requiring complete replacement.

analogies.analogy: Like fashion trends - clothes that are still functional become 'out of style' and are replaced, creating waste.
Like a new electric car charger that can also charge old model cars - supporting previous versions while introducing new technology.
Like a lightbulb designed to burn out after a specific number of hours rather than lasting indefinitely.
Like keeping an old typewriter in an office because the cost of retraining staff on computers exceeds the benefits.
